title: Response of a Linear System
summary: This page discusses the properties of linear systems, specifically homogeneity, additivity, and superposition, expressed mathematically through scaling and summing of inputs and outputs. It introduces the concept that a linear system's total response is the sum of the zero-input response (ZIR) and the zero-state response (ZSR). A drill exercise demonstrates that a system outputting the real part of a complex input satisfies additivity but not homogeneity, thus is not linear.
